---
title: "Courses"
subtitle: "Course Supervision and Coordination"
title-block-banner: false
---

## Undergraduate Study

### Principles of Computer Graphics and Image Processing

**Undergraduate level course**

Fundamental principles of computer graphics, image representation, processing algorithms, and visualization techniques.

#### Topics Covered

- Computer graphics fundamentals
- Image representation and color models
- 2D and 3D transformations
- Rendering techniques
- Image processing algorithms
- Graphics programming
- Visualization methods

### [Student Work](courses/principles-computer-graphics-student-work.qmd)

---

## Graduate Study

### Team Project

**Graduate level course**

Collaborative software development project where students work in teams to design, implement, and deliver a complex software system.

#### Focus Areas

- Team collaboration and communication
- Project management
- Software development lifecycle
- Code review and quality assurance
- Documentation and presentation
- Real-world problem solving

### [Student Work](courses/team-project-student-work.qmd)

---

### Advanced Computer Graphics Methods

**Graduate level course**

The course focuses on providing theoretical background and understanding of advanced computer graphics techniques. Gain insight into the design and implementation of computer graphics applications that rely on the mastery of the GPU. The goal is to master the basic principles and algorithms behind rendering of photorealistic 3D scenes in real time.

- **Credits:** 6
- **Assessment:** Semestral tasks in courses (20b), Project (20b), Exam (60b)

#### Course Contents

1. Photorealistic rendering
2. Raytracing, Radiosity, Monte-Carlo
3. Procedural texturing
4. Shadow casting
5. Image based rendering
6. BRDF, models for lighting and materials
7. Volumetric rendering
8. Augmented reality and Virtual reality
9. Lightfield theory and stereoscopic rendering
10. Vertex/fragment shader programming

### [Student Work](courses/advanced-computer-graphics-student-work.qmd)

---

### Data Visualization

**Graduate level course**

The goal of this course is to familiarize students with the basic and advanced techniques of information visualization and scientific visualization, which are the basis of visual analytics. Students learn key techniques of the visualization process including data preparation, visual mapping, and visualization itself — useful for understanding large-scale abstract data, scientific data and processes.

- **Credits:** 6
- **Assessment:** Semester project (60%), mid-term test (10%), final written exam (30%)

#### Course Contents

1. Introduction to the topic
2. Visual perception, visual representation of data, Gestalt principles, information overload
3. Creating visual representations, visualization reference model, visual mapping, visual analytics
4. Design of visualization techniques, architecture of visualization systems, design patterns
5. Classification of visualization systems
6. Interaction and distortion techniques
7. Visualization of one-, two- and multi-dimensional data, text and text documents
8. Visualization of groups, trees, graphs, clusters, networks, software
9. Metaphorical visualization
10. Visualization of volumetric data, vector fields, processes and simulations
11. Visualization of maps, geographic information, GIS systems
12. Collaborative visualizations
13. Evaluating visualizations

### [Student Work](courses/data-visualization-student-work.qmd)

---
